key_insight: An interpretable vision transformer can classify fracture origins in zirconia-toughened alumina from SEM images, and low-magnification views may already contain enough diagnostic information.
engineering_relevance: This can speed up ceramic-implant fracture analysis and quality assurance by enabling low-magnification pre-screening before time-intensive high-magnification inspection.
method: Curated 8,493 SEM fracture images from five years of burst and proof tests.
Fine-tuned a vision transformer to classify three defect origins: green body, hard machining, and material defects.
Used Grad-CAM plus leakage checks to verify interpretability and specimen separation.
evidence_signal: In stratified five-fold cross-validation, the model achieved 0.907 accuracy and 0.888 macro-F1, with low-magnification (50x) performance comparable to 1k-10kx.
watch_item: Results come from a single implant material system and should be validated more broadly before deployment across different ceramics, labs, and acquisition settings.
paper_title: Low-Magnification SEM May Suffice: Interpretable Deep Learning for Multi-Scale Fracture-Cause Classification in Zirconia-Toughened Alumina
